slides.title: The humble onion has a *quiet superpower*.
slides.sub: Quercetin is a plant compound found in onions and capers. Researchers are studying it for how the body ages.

slides.title: Meet quercetin
slides.body: Quercetin is a flavonoid: a colorful plant compound. It is one of the most studied antioxidants in the everyday diet.

slides.title: Cells that won't quit
slides.body: As we age, some cells stop dividing but refuse to die. These *senescent cells* linger and signal stress to their neighbors.

slides.title: The slow burn of aging
slides.body: Those lingering cells can fuel low-grade, chronic inflammation. That quiet inflammation *is linked to* much of how we age.

slides.title: A senolytic candidate
slides.body: In early research, quercetin acts as a senolytic: a compound that *may help clear* worn-out cells and calm inflammation.

slides.title: Easy to eat more
slides.body: Red onions, capers, apples and kale are rich sources. Small, steady additions to real meals *add up over time*.

slides.title: Small foods, *long game*.
slides.body: Vibe Health turns the food, sleep, movement and steps behind healthy aging into one daily Health Score, so the small choices stay easy to keep.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
The onion in your fridge might be doing more than flavoring dinner. 🧅🌿

Quercetin is a flavonoid in onions, capers and apples that researchers are studying as a senolytic: it may help clear worn-out 'zombie' cells and quiet the chronic inflammation behind aging. Save this for your next grocery run. 🔖

Vibe Health turns the food and habits behind healthy aging into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Quercetin: The Onion Compound Scientists Study for Aging
